mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels in >55% of monthly observations over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.2 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, triggering short-term positive equity momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to generate statistically significant positive 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10) during post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 315.0 coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in >50% of monthly observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm labor market tightening and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC for 20-day forward returns across multiple expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product levels indicate robust aggregate economic activity, directly boosting corporate profit expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns (t-stat < 1.5) in quarterly walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal manufacturing contraction and weakening economic momentum, prompting equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Two consecutive months of falling INDPRO fail to predict negative 20-day S&P 500 returns with a directional accuracy below 45% in historical backtests.
